title: Fix red GitHub Actions CI after x86 smoke commit
description: The main branch run for commit 11a0764 is red. Failing jobs include stale/misaligned checks for Microkit SDK download, Ed25519 smoke check, HURD VM panel UI, TCP/IP smoke check, Phase 5 contract tests, and boot tests. Diagnose current workflow failures, update repo code or CI checks to match active AGENTS.md constraints, and push a greenable fix.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions for main no longer fails on stale checks and local equivalent gates pass before pushing.
close_reason: Added the missing llvm package for CI QEMU build jobs so llvm-objcopy is available, after run 25153295299 exposed that runner dependency gap.

title: Rebuild fault-injection CI on current agentOS boot path
description: The previous GitHub Actions fault-injection job used qemu_virt_riscv64, scripts/run-fault-inject-tests.sh, and EXTRA_PDS=fault_inject. In the current tree, EXTRA_PDS/FAULT_INJECT are not wired into the top-level/kernel Makefiles, fault_inject.elf is not added to IMAGES, and the script launches QEMU/serial paths that do not match the maintained xtask qemu-test boot path. The stale CI job was removed while fixing red main. Reintroduce fault-injection CI only after the PD is contract/build/topology wired and commands are delivered through the real IPC/agent control path.
acceptance_criteria: A current-board fault-injection image build path includes fault_inject.elf/topology explicitly; CI drives OP_FAULT_INJECT through a real IPC/control channel rather than appending to logs; the runner reuses maintained QEMU conventions; local and GitHub CI tests pass.

title: Fix x86_64 service PD runtime faults after root smoke boot
description: The x86_64 QEMU path now boots seL4 and reaches the root-task marker [rt] boot complete, but serial output after that marker still reports service PD VM faults. The current smoke test intentionally accepts the root marker while this is investigated separately. Observed faults include PCs near 0x400048, 0x400003e, and 0x403b40 with fault addrs near 0, 0x10004000, and 0x10001018. This is likely x86-specific service topology/shared-memory/MMIO setup after reusing the AArch64 service descriptor.
acceptance_criteria: make test TARGET_ARCH=x86_64 reaches the controller agentOS boot complete marker without service PD fault endpoint reports, or the x86 reduced smoke scope is documented with a narrower board topology.

title: Wire x86_64 QEMU boot path for flat agentos.img
description: make test TARGET_ARCH=x86_64 can build the image, but QEMU cannot boot build/x86_64_generic/agentos.img with -kernel because agentos.img is the custom flat container, not an ELF/bzImage/multiboot payload. xtask qemu-test now treats x86_64_generic + GUEST_OS=none as a build-only gate and reports that limitation explicitly. Follow-up needs a real x86 loader/multiboot path for the flat image, plus an x86-specific system descriptor instead of falling through to system_desc_riscv64.c.
acceptance_criteria: make test TARGET_ARCH=x86_64 launches QEMU and observes the agentOS boot marker; docs/boot-guide.md and boards/qemu-x86_64/board.mk describe a valid boot command; root-task x86 builds use an x86_64 system descriptor or a documented shared descriptor intentionally.
close_reason: Implemented x86_64 QEMU boot through seL4 sel4_32.elf with root_task.elf as initrd, embedded the PD bundle for x86, added an x86 root-task stack shim, suppressed libsel4 TLS in freestanding builds, installed x86 paging-object retry support, fixed x86 child stack alignment, and updated xtask/board/docs. make test TARGET_ARCH=x86_64 now reaches [rt] boot complete. Follow-up service PD runtime faults are tracked in agentos-9qe.

title: Add missing tests/api/ unit tests for 6 production root-task APIs
description:
Identified during agentos-0mz investigation 2026-04-29. The following root-task APIs have production callers but no unit test in tests/api/:

| Production API           | Callers                                    | Missing test file        |
|--------------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------|
| ut_alloc / ut_alloc_cap  | kernel/agentos-root-task/src/{main,pd_tcb}.c | tests/api/test_ut_alloc.c |
| pd_vspace_create/load_elf| widespread                                  | tests/api/test_pd_vspace.c |
| cap_acct_record/count    | main.c:824,826 cap_accounting.c:60,62,64    | tests/api/test_cap_accounting.c |
| ep_alloc                 | exists in include/ep_alloc.h                | tests/api/test_ep_alloc.c |
| vfs_server/virtio_blk/block_pd | the storage stack                      | tests/api/test_storage_stack.c |
| nameserver               | main.c uses heavily; tests/contracts/nameserver_test.c is contract-level only | tests/api/test_nameserver.c |

The deleted worktree-agent branches had test files for some of these (1165+891+657 lines for nameserver alone) but they target branch-only APIs and cannot be salvaged. New tests must be written against main's actual signatures.

Per CLAUDE.md: 'Every opcode in every contract must have at least one test.' Recommend tackling in priority order: cap_accounting (6 prod call sites, simplest), ut_alloc (foundational), nameserver (heavily used), then pd_vspace/ep_alloc/storage.
close_reason: Added tests/api suites for cap_accounting, ut_alloc, ep_alloc, pd_vspace, nameserver, and storage stack; run_api_tests plus aarch64/x86_64 gates pass. Filed agentos-hwc for the separate x86 flat-image QEMU boot path limitation.

title: Six standalone worktree-agent branches: lost test files and unmerged xtask subcommands
description:
All six standalone branches were inspected on 2026-04-29 and deleted because their bodies were superseded by commits already in main, while their additive content (test files, xtask subcommands) couldn't compile against main.

Reflog preserves these for ~90 days:

| Branch       | Reflog SHA | Lost additive content                                          |
|--------------|------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| a0dbf18...   | 5de77b9    | tests/api/test_storage_stack.c (578 lines)                    |
| a71da416...  | d1a81fb    | (none — fully superseded; cmd_gen_image.rs already in main)   |
| a8578e7c...  | 93ae8d9    | tests/harness/sel4_test_harness.c (254), test_harness_self.c (91), xtask/src/cmd_run_tests.rs (229) — alternate test harness |
| a8b8c56c...  | f685dd6    | xtask/src/cmd_gen_caps.rs (270 lines) — novel CNode allocation plan generator |
| ab1366df...  | 28ee68d    | tests/api/test_ep_alloc.c (398 lines)                         |
| ac19d4c7...  | 49ec085    | libs/libvmm/include/vmm_caps.h (159 lines) — note: main has libvmm/ (no libs/ prefix) so the path itself is wrong |

Of these, two items are worth re-investigating later:

1. **xtask gen-caps** (a8b8c56c): novel CNode allocation plan generator that doesn't exist in main. Per CLAUDE.md API-first mandate this would help validate cap budgets at build time.
2. **xtask run-tests + sel4_test_harness.c** (a8578e7c): main has tests/harness/{sel4_test_harness.h, test_framework.h, test_runner.c} but no xtask wrapper. The wrapper would let CI invoke the seL4-target tests uniformly.

The test files themselves (test_storage_stack, test_ep_alloc) reference branch-only APIs and would need rewriting against main's actual APIs.
close_reason: Resolved salvage pass against current main. The storage stack and endpoint allocator API tests are now present in tests/api and passing. The remaining deleted xtask gen-caps/run-tests bodies are not drop-in compatible with the current agentos.toml and qemu-test flow; follow-ups were filed as agentos-r8d and agentos-3sc instead of merging stale tooling.

title: Three duplicated tests/api/test_nameserver.c implementations were not salvaged
description:
Three deleted worktree-agent branches each added their own tests/api/test_nameserver.c targeting different opcodes:

- a639076b (deleted): 1165-line test file (full nameserver dispatch coverage)
- a9f9b727 (deleted): 657-line test file (OP_NS_REGISTER, OP_NS_LOOKUP)
- aa2d6ced (deleted): 891-line test file (OP_NS_REVOKE)

Plus a9f9b727 sketched an alternate userspace/servers/nameserver/ architecture (605-line src/nameserver.c, 102-line nameserver_internal.h, 30-line CMakeLists.txt) instead of the kernel/agentos-root-task placement that landed in main.

None of the test files compile against main's nameserver.h API. Reflog has commits 6a9c0d9, 244294a, 4175096 for ~90 days.

Main's nameserver coverage today: tests/contracts/nameserver_test.c. If gaps exist (REGISTER/LOOKUP/REVOKE edge cases), this is the work to mine for them.
close_reason: Expanded current tests/api/test_nameserver.c with the salvageable edge cases from the deleted branches: full registry, lookup/update/deregister not-found paths, gated lookup not-found, empty LIST shmem, and re-register after deregister. Also aligned nameserver_contract.h with the production opcode/error ABI and verified it compiles standalone. The branch-only OP_NS_REVOKE design was not merged because that opcode is not in the active NameServer contract.

title: Investigate richer ut_alloc/pd_vspace/cap_acct APIs lost from worktree-agent branches
description:
Four worktree-agent branches (a309f0a3, acf4d3c0, ade901a4, aea01ddb) implemented richer kernel APIs than what landed in main. Their test files reference symbols absent from main:

- ut_alloc.h: branches expose ut_alloc(), main only exposes ut_advance_slot_cursor()
- pd_vspace.h: branches expose pd_vspace_init(), pd_vspace_map_frame(), pd_vspace_set_scratch_va(); main only has pd_vspace_create() and pd_vspace_load_elf()
- cap_accounting.h: branches expose cap_acct_count_pd(), cap_acct_revoke_pd(); main only has cap_acct_init() and cap_acct_record()

The test suites (test_ut_alloc.c 493 lines, test_pd_vspace.c 851 lines, test_cap_accounting.c 584 lines) cannot compile against main and were therefore not merged when the branches were cleaned up on 2026-04-29. The branches were deleted but the work history is in reflog for ~90 days.

Deciding whether to extend main's APIs to match the branch versions, port the tests, and re-add cap_acct_revoke_pd() (used for VOS_DESTROY) is a real design call.
notes:
Investigation complete 2026-04-29.

Original bead premise was wrong: my regex for header function decls was too strict and missed seL4_Error / int / void return types. Main's APIs are RICHER than the deleted branches:

ut_alloc.h:    main has 9 functions (alloc, alloc_cap, alloc_slot, alloc_device_*x3, advance_slot_cursor, free_slot_base, init); branch had 3
pd_vspace.h:   main has 4 (create, load_elf, map_region, map_device_frame); branch had 5 with different abstraction (init, create, load_elf, map_frame, set_scratch_va) — no clean overlap
cap_accounting: main has init/record/count/get; branch had init/record/count_pd/revoke_pd. Per-PD revocation in main is done via cap_tree_walk_pd + services/vibeos/vos_destroy.c instead — different design, equivalent capability
cap_tree.h:    main and branch have IDENTICAL function set (init, insert, remove, walk_pd, find_cap)

Production callers verified:
  ut_alloc → pd_tcb.c:47, main.c:84 (and many more)
  cap_acct_record → main.c:824,826 + cap_accounting.c:60,62,64

The real gap is test coverage for these production APIs. See follow-up bead.
close_reason: investigation complete; APIs are fine; real issue is missing test coverage tracked in follow-up bead

title: Design seL4-target TAP runner before salvaging xtask run-tests
description: Deleted branch commit 93ae8d9 added xtask run-tests plus a separate sel4_test_harness.c, but it assumed an agentos-test.img and a standalone qemu-system-aarch64 TAP sentinel flow that main does not currently build. Main already has host API TAP tests and xtask qemu-test. Salvage this only after defining how seL4-target tests are built and booted in the current tree.
acceptance_criteria: A seL4-target test image build path exists, the runner reuses current board/QEMU conventions where practical, TAP_DONE success/failure is documented, and cargo tests or a scripted smoke test cover timeout and pass/fail parsing.

title: Rebuild xtask gen-caps for current agentos.toml schema
description: Deleted branch commit f685dd6 added an xtask gen-caps command, but it expected a stale system TOML schema with [[pds]], [[mrs]], and [[endpoints]]. Main now feeds gen-image and gen-pd-bundle from the smaller [[pd]] agentos.toml schema, while root-task slot allocation is implemented directly in C. Reintroduce gen-caps only after aligning it with the active descriptor and cap layout.
acceptance_criteria: cargo xtask gen-caps consumes kernel/agentos-root-task/agentos.toml and board override TOMLs, emits a deterministic cap layout header matching root-task slot allocation constants, and has cargo tests covering at least one minimal descriptor.

title: libmicrokit_compat shim was scoped but never integrated
description:
Branch worktree-agent-aaa95813 (deleted 2026-04-29) added libs/libmicrokit_compat (465 lines: CMakeLists.txt + microkit.h + microkit_compat.c) providing a Microkit→raw seL4 IPC compatibility shim:

  microkit_ppcall, microkit_notify, microkit_irq_ack, microkit_get_variable
  + microkit_compat_register_channel/irq/variable setup helpers

Main has zero callers using the shim — all current microkit references are in libvmm/dep/sddf/ (third-party tree) and tests/. Per CLAUDE.md (no features beyond what the task requires) the shim was not salvaged.

If a future migration wants to gradually port libvmm or test code off Microkit, this shim is in reflog (commit 92cfdda).
close_reason: Closed after rechecking current main and the deleted shim. The raw-seL4 VMM capability path is already represented by libvmm/include/libvmm/vmm_caps.h and libvmm callers; the deleted libmicrokit_compat shim has no current integration point and would reintroduce a broad Microkit compatibility layer without an active caller. No code was merged.
